title: Byron's letters
body: The letters of Lord Byron, of which about 3,000 are known, range in date from 1798, when Byron was 10 years old, to 9 April 1824, a few days before he died.  They have long received extraordinary critical praise for their wit, spontaneity and sincerity.  Many rate Byron as the greatest letter-writer in English literature, and consider his letters comparable or superior to his poems as literary achievements.  They have also been called "one of the three great informal autobiographies in English", alongside the diaries of Samuel Pepys and James Boswell.  Their literary value is reflected in the huge prices collectors will pay for them; in 2009 a sequence of 15 letters to his friend Francis Hodgson was sold at auction for almost £280,000.
